title: physanthyllis tetraphylla
intro: Physanthyllis tetraphylla, also known as the "Rat's Tail Plant", is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the Bignoniaceae family. It is native to Mexico and Central America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and roots contain compounds that have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, contributing to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestive health. Physanthyllis tetraphylla can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with medications.
